fault_hypothesis: Lines 77-78 incorrectly pop the last merged element when the last element of left, right, and merged are all equal. This erroneously removes a valid duplicate entry that should be preserved in the output.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition on line 77 should never be true because popping a valid duplicate entry is incorrect; this expression is false whenever the bug triggers.
After each loop iteration the merged list should have at most i+j elements (one per consumed element from either stream).
After popping, the merged list should still contain at least len(left)+len(right)-1 elements, but the bug causes it to have fewer than expected.
Before any post-processing, merged should contain exactly len(left)+len(right) elements since every element from both inputs must be preserved.
